content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Both amendments identify real defects in the iterative_minimal workflow engine. fr-7-656cf9e6 is a clear performance bug: the filesystem-fallback fingerprint ignores the target/target-final/result* filter that the git path applies, so in a Rust project without git the fallback hashes multi-GB artifact trees every iteration. fr-7-13e645ad identifies a real cost/correctness issue: a successful backend invocation is discarded (and billed again on resume) when the sidecar atomic write fails, because persist_invocation_parsed_payload's `?` escapes the and_then closure before implementer_iteration_completed is journaled.

## Votes

- `fr-7-656cf9e6`: Accept
  Verified in engine.rs: hash_workspace_entries (6369-6412) only calls iterative_fingerprint_excludes_path, which matches just `.git` and `.ralph-burning`. The git path (hash_untracked_git_entry at 6505-6516) additionally calls iterative_untracked_build_artifact to skip `target`, `target-final`, and `result*`. When git_repo_available returns false, workspace_diff_fingerprint (6414-6418) walks the entire workspace — including target/ — before and after every implementer iteration. For typical Rust projects this is multi-GB of SHA-256 hashing per iteration, turning an O(backend-call) loop into something dominated by filesystem IO. The existing test (workflow_engine_test.rs:1807) only uses setup_workspace with no target/, so the bug is not exercised. The proposed fix (apply iterative_untracked_build_artifact filtering in hash_workspace_entries) is straightforward, and adding a test with a populated target/ directory is appropriate coverage.
- `fr-7-13e645ad`: Accept
  Verified in engine.rs: invoke_stage_on_backend (6897-6917) uses .and_then with `persist_invocation_parsed_payload(...)?;` inside — so any StageCommitFailed from the atomic write escapes and the bundle is dropped. The iterative loop at 6756-6784 then journals implementer_loop_exited reason="error" (without ever writing implementer_iteration_completed at 6792-6813). On resume, reconstruct_iterative_state_from_events (1215-1252) only counts ImplementerIterationCompleted events, so completed_iterations=N-1 and iteration N is reinvoked. Since the parsed-payload sidecar was never written, even if the run later terminates at iteration N, recover_iterative_iteration_result (6187-6236) cannot reload the earlier bundle — the backend is billed twice for identical work. A transient IO error on the sidecar path shouldn't convert a completed, valid bundle into a failed run. Option 1 (warn + continue with in-memory bundle, accepting that terminal recovery may re-invoke) is indeed the least-invasive fix given the ordering constraints the amendment rules out; it restores the happy path while degrading gracefully on disk pressure.
